overview: Jeskai Energy in Modern leverages the energy mechanic to rapidly accelerate into large, resilient threats while maintaining board presence and card advantage. The deck uses cheap early plays to establish energy generation, then transitions into massive creatures that can overwhelm opponents through combat or direct damage. Interaction and graveyard recursion provide the necessary resilience to outlast aggressive strategies and close out games against control.
win_conditions: Overwhelming combat damage with pumped Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury
Direct damage from energy spenders and creature abilities
Card advantage and board control via Nulldrifter and Doorkeeper Thrull

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and an early energy generator or Nulldrifter
Hands containing Doorkeeper Thrull paired with removal or energy acceleration
Hands that can play a creature on turn one or two
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking lands or early plays
Hands with only late-game energy spenders and no generators
Hands with multiple high-cost cards and no ramp

early_game: Prioritize playing Nulldrifter to establish an early board presence and protect energy generators
Begin generating energy immediately to fuel Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury's growth
Use cheap interaction to disrupt opponent's early game
mid_game: Bring in Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and pump it using accumulated energy
Activate Doorkeeper Thrull to exile opponent's graveyard threats or prevent lethal damage
Maintain board pressure while cycling through energy resources
late_game: Convert excess energy into massive combat damage or direct damage
Use Doorkeeper Thrull's recursion to refill hand and maintain card advantage
Close out games with unblockable or oversized creatures

key_cards.name: Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and energy sink
key_cards.notes: Generate energy before attacking to pump it, then use its sacrifice ability to deal direct damage or finish the game

key_cards.name: Nulldrifter
key_cards.role: Early game board presence and tempo play
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to establish a flying threat that forces opponent to answer early, buying time for energy generation

key_cards.name: Doorkeeper Thrull
key_cards.notes: Use its ability to exile opponent's key cards from the graveyard or prevent lethal damage, then reuse it by returning it from exile

sequencing_tips: Generate energy before declaring attackers to maximize Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury's size
Hold up energy for Doorkeeper Thrull's damage prevention before committing to all-in attacks
Prioritize playing energy generators before spenders to avoid stalling
Use Nulldrifter to block or pressure while setting up energy for turn three

matchups.advice: Play Doorkeeper Thrull early to prevent damage and disrupt their graveyard recursion, then race with pumped Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury
Use Nulldrifter to force early answers, then flood the board with energy-fueled threats that are hard to remove efficiently
Disrupt their key pieces with Doorkeeper Thrull's exile ability and apply early pressure with Nulldrifter to prevent them from assembling their combo
Race their value engines by pumping Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury quickly and using energy to out-tempo their removal

common_threats: Board wipes that clear energy investments
Fast aggro creatures that pressure before energy ramps
Graveyard hate that disrupts Doorkeeper Thrull
